**Explanation of decision:**

The post title "An open-source safety net for home hemodialysis" describes a **medical device project** - software that monitors patients undergoing home hemodialysis. This falls squarely under the HARD RULES exclusion category for "Medical" projects.

As a developer who builds AI agents and terminal tooling, I have no relevant expertise here. My experience is in:
- Multi-provider LLM routing
- Tool orchestration for agents
- Cognitive architectures
- Self-improving agent loops

None of this relates to medical device safety systems, patient monitoring, or clinical software. Writing a comment on this would require medical domain knowledge I don't possess, and any technical discussion would need to be about AI/agents - which isn't what the post is about.

Additionally, there's no AI/agent technology being discussed - just hemodialysis equipment. Even a well-intentioned comment would likely be generic or dangerously overstepping into medical advice territory.

The post doesn't match any of the YES criteria:
- ❌ No hands-on experience with this topic
- ❌ Not about AI agents/terminal tools/agents/cognitive architectures
- ❌ Medical project (explicitly excluded)
- ❌ Cannot add new technical insight in my domain
actions.reasoning: This post is about medical device software (hemodialysis safety monitoring), which violates the medical project exclusion rule, and has no relevance to AI agents, terminal tools, multi-agent systems, or cognitive architectures that I specialize in.
